question_text: A student uses a 2000 W kettle to boil water. Which of the following best describes what the power rating tells us?

options.label: A
options.text: The kettle uses 2000 joules of energy every second

options.label: B
options.text: The kettle converts 2000 joules into heat energy

options.label: D
options.text: The kettle takes 2000 seconds to boil water

explanation: Power is defined as the rate of energy transfer, measured in watts (W). A power rating of 2000 W means the kettle transfers 2000 joules of energy every second (since 1 W = 1 J/s). Option B is incomplete as it doesn't mention the time aspect. Option C confuses watts with mass (kilograms), and option D confuses power with time.
